response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled full-stack and systems engineer with broad polyglot experience, but he critically fails the mandatory requirements for this role. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing components for an IDE, plus a bachelor thesis side project — nowhere near the required 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle expertise, no Windows-platform development context, and no fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge. His only finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) used TypeScript and GraphQL, not C++.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) transferable to disciplined financial-systems engineering
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems at Dunnhumby demonstrates performance-oriented thinking relevant to capital-markets infrastructure
Java background and hands-on CI/CD pipeline experience satisfy two of the optional desirable criteria
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months total) — the single most critical mandatory requirement of 5–10 years is substantially unmet
No demonstrated Windows-platform development and no SQL Server or Oracle proficiency anywhere in the resume
Zero fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets domain knowledge
